name: Parque Natural de Oyambre – circular desde Comillas por el Monte Corona y Punta Lumbreras
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Comillas offer diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to elevated inland routes. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Cantabrian Sea, featuring rolling dunes, cliffs, and estuaries within the Oyambre Nature Park. Mountain bikers can expect varied terrain, including lush green hills, forested sections, and routes that traverse both paved and unpaved surfaces. The area provides a mix of moderate climbs and descents, making it suitable for riders seeking a balance of challenge and scenic exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Comillas The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de Oyambre – circular desde Comillas por el Monte Corona y Punta Lumbreras, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through a natural park, featuring elevated areas like Monte Corona and Punta Lumbreras. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Historic center of Comillas – El Capricho de Gaudí loop from Comillas, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.9 km) path. This route combines cultural sightseeing with cycling through varied terrain, including the historic center of Comillas. Local mountain bikers also love the El Capricho de Gaudí – Historic center of Comillas loop from Comillas, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail leading through the historic center and notable landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Comillas is defined by coastal paths, natural parks, and lush green hills, offering routes for various ability levels. The network includes options from easier rides to more technically challenging sections. The routes in Comillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Comillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Comillas?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Comillas, offering a variety of experiences. The majority of these routes are classified as moderate, with a few easier options available for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Comillas?
A: Yes, Comillas offers some easier mountain bike routes. While most trails are moderate, you can find options with well-maintained roads and gentle slopes. For instance, the region research mentions "Route 8: Bike tour of Comillas" as a low-difficulty option, though some sections might have mud or steeper inclines. When planning, look for routes described as having lower elevation gains and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Comillas?
A: The terrain around Comillas is quite diverse, ranging from coastal paths with rolling dunes and cliffs to lush green hills and forested sections inland. You'll encounter a mix of asphalt, tracks, and wider paths. Many routes traverse the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied landscapes and natural features.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints in Comillas?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Comillas provide stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000033768/parque-natural-de-oyambre-circular-desde-comillas-por-el-monte-corona-y-punta-lumbreras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Oyambre – circular desde Comillas por el Monte Corona y Punta Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, takes you through elevated areas like Monte Corona and Punta Lumbreras. Beyond the trails, viewpoints like Mirador de Santa Lucía and the Comillas Cemetery also offer magnificent perspectives of the coast and town.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking near Comillas?
A: Comillas is rich in cultural and natural attractions. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt; and past architectural marvels like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Gaudí&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1507066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rabia Estuary&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2589035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolao Cliff&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas – El Capricho de Gaudí loop from Comillas&lt;/a&gt;, specifically combine cycling with sightseeing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Comillas?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Comillas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000033768/parque-natural-de-oyambre-circular-desde-comillas-por-el-monte-corona-y-punta-lumbreras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Oyambre – circular desde Comillas por el Monte Corona y Punta Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sequoia Forest loop from Cementerio de Ruiseñada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the average duration for mountain bike trails in Comillas?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Comillas varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate routes typically range from 2 to 3 hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000033768/parque-natural-de-oyambre-circular-desde-comillas-por-el-monte-corona-y-punta-lumbreras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Oyambre loop&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24640393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Gaudí – Historic center of Comillas loop&lt;/a&gt; can take nearly 3 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Comillas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Comillas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, which include coastal paths, natural parks, and lush green hills, offering a great balance of challenge and scenic exploration. The combination of cultural sights with natural beauty is also frequently highlighted.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Comillas?
A: Comillas, being a coastal town in Cantabria, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking outside of the peak summer heat and potentially wetter winter months. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less precipitation. While winter riding is possible, some sections might be muddier or have steeper inclines due to weather conditions.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that explore unique natural areas like a forest?
A: Yes, you can find routes that take you through unique natural environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sequoia Forest loop from Cementerio de Ruiseñada&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a moderate ride through a distinctive forest setting, providing a different experience from the coastal trails.

Q: What are the starting points for mountain bike trails in Comillas?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Comillas conveniently start directly from the town itself, or from nearby villages. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas – View of Comillas loop from Comillas&lt;/a&gt; begin right in Comillas, making them easily accessible. Other routes might start from specific points like the Cementerio de Ruiseñada for the Sequoia Forest loop.
tips: At the top of Monte Corona is the hermitage of San Esteban, a place frequented by those of us who enjoy riding along good forest tracks and ending at a viewpoint with beautiful panoramic views.
Beautiful hermitage located on the slopes of Monte Corona
On one side of the road is this viewpoint to the oyambre of the La Rabia river. Respect the signs, you have to dismount from the bike the few meters along the path that are really worth it.
This stretch of coastal bike path is very picturesque and is usually quiet, not very crowded, which allows you to appreciate the beautiful views of the Punta Lumbreras coast.
